description:
Convert the eight `FnId.0`-keyed codegen structures to `SpecId.0`-keyed AND enable per-callsite narrow-spec consumption. These two changes are coupled: structural conversion only delivers value once multiple specs per fn exist; narrow consumption only works once the structures accommodate multiple specs.

Combined into one atom so the commit's diff tells a coherent story.

## Concretely

Eight structures convert from FnId.0-indexed to SpecId.0-indexed:
- `schemas: Vec<Schema>`
- `fn_sigs: Vec<Signature>`
- `fn_ids: HashMap<SpecId, FuncId>`
- `fn_ptrs: HashMap<SpecId, *const u8>` (in CompiledModule)
- `frame_sizes: Vec<u32>`
- `return_descrs: Vec<Descr>`
- `parking_reachable: HashSet<SpecId>`
- `natively_callable: HashSet<SpecId>`

Narrow-spec consumption:
- For every non-any-key spec in `module_types.specs`, register an additional SpecId via `spec_registry.register(fn_id, input_descrs)`.
- Build Schema / Signature / FuncId / frame_size for each new SpecId. Compile each via `compile_fn` against the matching FnTypes.
- At Term::Call / Term::TailCall / cont-frame allocation sites: compute the actual arg-Descr tuple from the caller's spec's FnTypes; resolve `(callee, arg_descrs)` → SpecId via `spec_registry`; fall back to any-key SpecId if no narrow spec exists. Emit the call against the resolved SpecId's FuncId.

## Runtime contract

SpecIds are dense u32 from 0..spec_count. `schema_id` in frame headers is now SpecId.0 (was FnId.0). The runtime side is unchanged because it already keys on opaque u32 schema_ids — it just sees more of them.

## Acceptance

- Non-any-key SpecIds are reachable from at least one Term::Call site.
- Fixture matrix green.
- CLIF diffs visible on typed-flow fixtures (fact, dist, add, etc.): narrowed bodies emit fewer `fz_alloc_float` and fewer cont-frame allocations.
- No FnId.0 indexing in codegen except at the callsite-resolution boundary (entering the SpecRegistry lookup).

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.2.
title: .29.2.1: Per-callsite narrow-spec consumption
